purpose: Known LCC holding accounts where vendor invoices land before being split via journal to destination cost centres. Tracing a passthrough cost requires identifying the holding account first, then following the split journal to its destinations.

accounts.purpose: Holding account for whole-zone landscape maintenance contracts (Austspray, Glascott). Vendor invoices post here as lump sums and are then journal-split across destination services using fixed values from the Green Maintenance Budget Tracker (DM# 17767639).
accounts.split_basis: Fixed dollar values from Green Maintenance Budget Tracker DM# 17767639. Values do not vary month-to-month and are not derived from any vendor document.
accounts.governance_doc: Urban Bushland Checklist DM# 14719249 — defines approved scope per zone but is NOT what drives the split.
accounts.known_issue: The Tracker split values were never reconciled against the Checklist. LZ6 split is 6× the Checklist-approved scope ($13,993/month vs $2,306/round). Annualised overcharge to PK385: ~$140,246. See worked_example_pk385.md.

tracing_rule: If a PK has zero direct vendor invoices but carries operational spend, it is downstream of a holding account. Find the upstream holding account by tracing GJ entries on the PK back to their source AP invoices.
common_journal_signatures: GJ followed by a debit/credit pair where one side is the holding account and the other is the destination PK (look for matching dollar amounts on opposite signs)
RJ at end of month — accrual for vendor invoice not yet processed; mirrors the same split values as the eventual GJ
DM reference in the narration (e.g. DM# 17767639 = Green Tracker) names the basis of the split
